Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: the typical Procure to Pay process follows this sequence 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Issue Purchase Order 4 Receive Goods Services 5 Vendor Invoice Received 6 Match Invoice to PO 3 way match PO receipt invoice 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Vendor Invoice Match Invoice Release Payment Close Case This looks perfect Follows the standard sequence exactly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Create PR Issue PO Vendor Invoice Release Payment Approve PR Receive Goods Match Invoice Close Case They issued a PO before getting approval for the PR then paid the vendor before receiving goods or matching the invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Release Payment Vendor Invoice Received Goods Match Invoice Close Case Here they released payment before receiving goods or getting the invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 Create PR Approve PR Receive Goods Issue PO Vendor Invoice Match Invoice Release Payment Close Case they received goods before issuing the PO

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For Case 1002 Issue PO before Approve PR That is dangerous because unapproved purchases could lead to unauthorized spending Release Payment before Receive Goods Paying without confirming receipt is risky Also Release Payment before Match Invoice you should always match invoice to PO receipt before paying

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For Case 1003 Release Payment before Receive Goods and before Vendor Invoice That is extremely risky You might pay for something you never received or that does not match the invoice

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Case 1004 Receive Goods before Issue PO This suggests either they received goods without a formal PO maverick buying or the PO was issued after receipt but backdated in the system

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Financial Risks Paying before receiving goods invoice means you might pay for undelivered items or incorrect amounts Paying without proper matching increases fraud risk fake invoices overpayments Compliance Issues Issuing POs without approved requisitions violates segregation of duties Receiving goods without a PO means no purchase authorization existed Operational Inefficiencies Back and forth sequences suggest process breakdowns or system issues Audit and Control Failures Standard 3 way match PO receipt invoice is bypassed making financial audits difficult

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: These suggest either deliberate circumvention of controls potential fraud system configuration issues where activities are recorded out of order lack of training on proper P2P procedures urgent situations where normal procedures were bypassed
